title: Slancha Blog
description: Technical deep dives, tutorials, and insights on AI engineering: model evaluation, deployment strategies, fine-tuning pipelines, and the eval-deploy-train loop.

items.title: Zero-Config AI Inference: Why the Black Box Wins
items.summary: Every AI infrastructure platform gives you more knobs. Slancha took them away. Here's why the black box approach to AI inference consistently outperforms teams with "full control" — and what the data says about how engineering teams actually manage model selection.

items.title: Introducing Slancha: The AI Inference Platform That Gets Better While You Sleep
items.summary: Today we're opening early access to Slancha — an end-to-end AI inference platform that routes, fine-tunes, and optimizes your LLM workloads behind a single API endpoint. No model selection, no benchmarking, no ML team required.

items.title: The Case for Black Box AI Inference: Why Your Team Should Stop Picking Models
items.summary: Every AI platform promises transparency and control. Slancha bets on the opposite: a black box that handles everything. Here's why that's the right call for 90% of teams using LLM APIs.

items.title: Slancha vs. Databricks: The AI Infrastructure Showdown
items.summary: Databricks gives you the tools. Slancha does the work. A detailed comparison of two fundamentally different approaches to AI infrastructure — full control vs. automatic results.

items.title: From Prototype to Production: The AI Deployment Checklist
items.summary: Most AI projects that "work" in prototype never make it to production. This checklist covers what actually breaks and how to fix it — routing, data curation, quantization, GPU efficiency, and more.

items.title: Building a Production AI Router: Architecture Patterns That Scale
items.summary: Routing requests to the right model is the easy part. The hard part is doing it at scale with sub-millisecond overhead, graceful degradation, and zero downtime deploys. Here are the architecture patterns that make it work.

items.title: The Complete Guide to AI Model Routing: Strategies, Architecture, and Cost Optimization
items.summary: Not every request needs GPT-4. Learn how intelligent model routing cuts inference costs 40-70% while maintaining quality — with architecture patterns, routing strategies, and real benchmarks.

items.title: How Eval Data Should Drive Fine-Tuning: A Technical Deep Dive
items.summary: A hands-on guide to building a closed-loop pipeline where evaluation failures automatically become training examples — with code, architecture patterns, and real metrics.

items.title: 5 Signs Your ML Team Needs an Evaluation Platform
items.summary: Spreadsheets, vibes-based deployment, and "it works on my laptop" are not an eval strategy. Here's how to know you've outgrown ad-hoc testing.

items.title: Why Eval Data Should Drive Fine-Tuning
items.summary: Most teams treat evaluation and fine-tuning as separate workflows. That disconnect is costing you model quality and engineering hours.

items.title: The Real Cost of Stitching AI Tools Together
items.summary: You're paying for 4-6 tools that don't talk to each other. The integration tax is higher than you think.

items.title: Introducing the Slancha Router: Free Intelligent Model Routing
items.summary: Route requests to the best model for the job — automatically. Free to use, no lock-in.

items.title: Slancha vs OpenRouter: Beyond the Model Marketplace
items.summary: OpenRouter gives you access to every model through one API. Slancha gives you one API that makes model selection irrelevant. A detailed comparison of two fundamentally different approaches to multi-model AI.

items.title: How to Reduce Your LLM API Costs by 60% Without Sacrificing Quality
items.summary: LLM API bills are growing faster than usage. Here are five proven techniques — from intelligent routing to automated fine-tuning — that cut costs dramatically while maintaining or improving output quality.

items.title: The Multi-Model Future: Why One LLM Won't Rule Them All
items.summary: The era of picking one model and routing everything through it is ending. MoE architectures, task-specific fine-tuning, and intelligent routing are converging on a multi-model future. Here's what that means for your AI stack.
